title: Hydra MCP per-agent rate-limit state
description: Rolling-window rate-limit counters for MCP tools that mutate external systems (e.g. hydra.file_ticket creating GitHub issues). Commander writes this on each rate-limited tool call. Spec: docs/specs/2026-04-17-main-agent-filing.md. The structure is deliberately simple: a single `limits` block declares the ceiling per tool, and `agents.<id>.<tool>` is a list of ISO-8601 UTC timestamps in the current window. Timestamps older than `limits.<tool>.window_sec` are evicted on every read.

limits.description: Declares the per-tool ceilings. Keys are tool names (dotless — e.g. 'file_ticket' for hydra.file_ticket).

window_sec.description: Rolling window length in seconds. A call is counted if its timestamp is within `now - window_sec`.

max_calls.description: Maximum calls permitted per agent within the rolling window.

agents.description: Per-agent call-time lists. Keys are authorized_agents[].id values. Each agent's entry maps tool names to arrays of ISO-8601 UTC timestamps; timestamps older than the tool's window_sec are evicted on every read.

items.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of a single call.
